javax.media.mscontrol.mixer
Interface MixerEvent

All Superinterfaces:
MediaEvent<MediaMixer>, ResourceEvent<MediaMixer>
public interface MixerEvent
extends ResourceEvent<MediaMixer>

Defines the events sent by a Mixer

Field Summary
static EventType ACTIVE_INPUTS_CHANGED
          The event indicates that the list of ActiveInputs has changed.
static EventType MOST_ACTIVE_INPUT_CHANGED
          The event indicates that the most ActiveInput has changed.
 
Fields inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.resource.ResourceEvent
MANUAL_TRIGGER, NO_QUALIFIER, RTC_TRIGGERED, STOPPED
 
Fields inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.MediaEvent
NO_ERROR
 
Method Summary
 Joinable[] getActiveInputs()
          Each entry in the returned array is a reference on a media object (usually a NetworkConnection) that is actively participating to the mix.
 
Methods inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.resource.ResourceEvent
getQualifier, getRTCTrigger
 
Methods inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.MediaEvent
getError, getErrorText, getEventType, getSource, isSuccessful
 

Field Detail

MOST_ACTIVE_INPUT_CHANGED

static final EventType MOST_ACTIVE_INPUT_CHANGED
The event indicates that the most ActiveInput has changed.
Note: This event is not generated by default. To activate its generation, see MediaMixer.ENABLED_EVENTS.

ACTIVE_INPUTS_CHANGED

static final EventType ACTIVE_INPUTS_CHANGED
The event indicates that the list of ActiveInputs has changed.
Notes:
  • This event is not generated by default. To activate its generation, see MediaMixer.ENABLED_EVENTS.
  • This event is not sent if MOST_ACTIVE_INPUT_CHANGED is sent.

Method Detail

getActiveInputs

Joinable[] getActiveInputs()
Each entry in the returned array is a reference on a media object (usually a NetworkConnection) that is actively participating to the mix.
The array is ordered by activity; the first entry is the most active input.
If the media is coming into the Mixer through a MixerAdapter, then this methods returns the MixerAdapter reference.
Note: That object may belong to another MediaSession, and may even have been unjoined in the meantime.
